stimulus: Mireya Soto, director of the cultural heritage program of the Pueblo of Acoma in New Mexico, helped develop the region’s first dual-language children’s book series, Keres ______ a community radio station, KACO 91.7, in 2012; and a high school Keres-language elective curriculum, in 2018.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The convention being tested is the punctuation of items in a complex series. A semicolon is conventionally used to separate items in a series when those items already contain internal commas. The semicolon after "2010" correctly separates the first item ("the region’s…2010") from the second item ("a community…2012"), while the commas around "Keres Voices" and "KACO 91.7" set off the supplementary appositive names of the book series and the radio station.

Choice A is incorrect because it relies on commas alone, blurring the structure of the complex series. Choice C is incorrect because the semicolon after "Voices" misplaces the boundary between items: it splits the book series’ date ("in 2010") from the appositive title "Keres Voices." Choice D has the same mispunctuation as C, treating "in 2010" as belonging to the second item rather than the first.
